session_hint: the DNS resolution timeout investigation in production
question: What was the root cause of the DNS resolution timeouts on the app servers?
turns: user

DNS resolution intermittently timing out on our production app servers. Affecting outbound API calls and S3 access. Root cause identified immediately: /etc/resolv.conf on every app server lists only a single upstream nameserver (10.0.0.2) with no fallback entries. That server has 5% packet loss. With no alternate to try, every dropped query results in a full timeout after the default 5-second wait.
user

Investigated DNS cache poisoning as a possibility since some queries resolve and some don't. All our DNS traffic is internal VPC, never exposed to the public internet. Poisoning is not plausible here.
user
Checked DNSSEC validation: our stub resolver is systemd-resolved, and DNSSEC validation is disabled in resolved.conf. Not the cause.
user
Tested whether a firewall rule was blocking port 53: confirmed via security group audit that UDP/TCP 53 is open between app servers and the DNS server subnet. Firewall not the cause.
user
Captured tcpdump on an app server during a timeout event: resolver sends query to 10.0.0.2, retransmits to the same IP twice more, never attempts any alternate. Confirms single-nameserver behavior.
user
Checked all 12 app servers: identical resolv.conf with a single nameserver entry. All were provisioned from the same Terraform module that hardcodes exactly one nameserver.
user
Secondary finding: the packet loss on 10.0.0.2 was caused by a misconfigured NIC ring buffer. Fixed that too, but it doesn't address the structural problem.
user
Fix: updated the Terraform module to write three nameservers (10.0.0.2, 10.0.0.3, 10.0.0.4) and added 'options rotate' to round-robin. Reprovisioned all 12 servers.
user
DNS timeouts gone. DNSSEC validation and firewall both definitively ruled out.
user
Filed a ticket to add a nameserver count assertion to our instance provisioning smoke test suite. Closing the incident.
